phrasal_verbs.meaning: put clothes in a wardrobe, etc
lift something from the floor, a table, etc
return something to where it was
give a part of sth to a group of people
find the solution to a problem, etc

A.title: Topic vocabulary (gap fill)
A.instructions: Complete the sentences with words from the box.

questions.sentence: The police will ...... the incident before making a decision.
Don't ...... that everyone agrees with your idea.

The teacher had to ...... us about the dangerous road.
They ...... to accept the offer because it was too expensive.
Too much noise can ...... problems with your neighbours.
If we leave now, we'll avoid the traffic and, as a ......, arrive early.
He tried to ...... that he had broken the window, but everyone saw him.

B.title: Circle the correct word
B.instructions: Choose the correct option in each sentence.

questions.sentence: In my ......, this is the most sensible option. **view / pressure**
After the party, the kitchen was ...... . **in a mess / in danger**
Please don't spread ...... without checking facts. **rumours / discussions**
She stayed calm even when she was ...... . **under pressure / by accident**
The manager ...... her team for their hard work. **praised / criticised**
It wasn't intentional — I did it ...... . **by mistake / in trouble**

questions.sentence: Can you ...... this box? It's on the floor.

......! There's a car coming fast.

D.title: Prepositional phrases
D.instructions: Complete each sentence with a phrase from the list.

questions.sentence: I deleted the file ...... when I clicked the wrong button.

E.title: Word patterns

F.title: Word formation
F.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the word in capitals.

questions.sentence: Thanks for your useful ...... during the meeting. (ADVICE)
I felt very ...... because the instructions were unclear. (CONFUSE)
Everyone must follow the rules, without ...... . (EXCEPT)

He was ...... enough to find his keys in the dark. (LUCK)
My ...... is to study in the morning. (PREFER)
The doctor's ...... was to rest for two days. (RECOMMEND)
Their ...... to apologise made the situation worse. (REFUSE)
We need a practical ...... to this issue. (SOLVE)
Her ...... was accepted by the whole group. (SUGGEST)

Which phrasal verb means 'be careful'?

If you have no milk left, you have ...... milk.

In my ......, this is an ideal solution.
